draftNote: 1 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Mr_Meat31 (midlane→offlane).

coaching.headline: A 30-minute objective stranglehold — every Fangtooth, Prime, and Orb was yours, towers 10-3, +22 VP.
coaching.team: This wasn't a fight win, it was a map win: you took the neutrals 24-14 and snowballed it into a 10-3 tower lead. Cuban's Sparrow gave you the carry to cash those leads in (16/1/1), and your anti-heal was on point — four Tainted items buried their Riktor at 0/5/6 so their one healer never mattered. The lever is that this was a clean five-stack executing the right win condition: keep playing for objectives off won fights instead of chasing kills.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: Even in a win, build the resist your matchup demands — Xeebs' Khaimera ran zero magic resist into a 3-magical enemy comp, and against a sharper team that's a free death window.

perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: Sparrow hard-carried at 16/1/1 — yours at every checkpoint vs Revenant, one death all game and 35k hero damage. Even with your Vanquisher spike not landing until ~25m, the lane was won outright before then; this win keyed off you.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Khaimera jungle, 7/5/12 — yours at every checkpoint vs Boris. You won it on play; just buy magic resist into their 3-magical comp next time, because that's the one thing that could have opened a death window in the late fights.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Iggy & Scorch, 4/1/7 and 40k into objectives — even early then yours from 15 minutes on vs Aurora, and your Wraith Leggings spike ~25m lined up with that takeover. Your zone control is exactly why every neutral fell your way.
perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: The Fey mid, 2/1/14 with a single death — the Gadget lane was even early and tipped yours from ~20 minutes on, right around your Wraith Leggings spike (~19m). Your peel and 14 assists let the carry farm kills safely.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Steel support, 0/4/19 — even early then yours from 15 minutes on vs Riktor, eating 46k damage and mitigating 48k as the frontline. Pure enabling, and your Tainted item helped shut their Riktor down.

threats: Chain-CC risk (4 hard-CC abilities, ~3.5s of lockdown): Riktor Riplash (pull), Riktor Shock Therapy (silence 0.8s), Riktor Skewer (suppress 1.25s), Gadget Security Gate (root 1s). Don't group tight into it — stagger the engage and hold mobility/cleanse for the key one.
Heal-stacking: Riktor. Anti-heal is mandatory against this comp — you brought none.
synergy: Your engage/CC: Iggy & Scorch Inferno (knockback). Your kills come from landing burst INSIDE that CC window — whoever lands it, the rest must follow up immediately.
You have sustain (Steel) — favor the longer fight where your heals out-grind them.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Bramble Patch slow → Harvest Nettles stacks → Untamed Growth DoT → Fly Trap pull ult
ourKits.winCondition: Fly Trap a clumped team and detonate AoE on the pull
ourKits.passive: takedowns spawn growths + grant move speed
ourKits.playWith: engage that clumps for her Fly Trap
ourKits.playAgainst: spread out vs the pull; she's squishy — dive her

ourKits.combo: Oil Spill → Molotov ignite → Flame Turret → Inferno knockback ult
ourKits.winCondition: layer fire zones on a choke/objective and burn the team
ourKits.playWith: CC that holds enemies in his fire
ourKits.playAgainst: don't fight in his zones — flank; kill the turret, dive him

ourKits.combo: Piercing Shot poke → Heightened Senses AS → Hail of Arrows zone → Inner Fire ult
ourKits.winCondition: ramp Relentless + AS and sustained-DPS the front down
ourKits.playWith: heavy peel — she's immobile and pure DPS
ourKits.playAgainst: she has zero escape — hard-engage her and she dies first

ourKits.combo: Bull Rush knockback → Force Shield wall → Shield Slam knockup+stun ult, Shield Bash %hp
ourKits.winCondition: peel the carry with walls/knockups or engage with Shield Slam
ourKits.playWith: a carry he can wall for and peel
ourKits.playAgainst: his Force Shield blocks projectiles — reposition around it; bait Shield Slam

ourKits.combo: Ambush leap → Unleash AS → Cull stun, Invigorate to cleanse+heal
ourKits.winCondition: stick on one target, stun with Cull and out-sustain via Spirit Regen
ourKits.playWith: follow-up burst inside his Cull stun
ourKits.playAgainst: ANTI-HEAL is mandatory — it guts his whole kit; kite the leap and CC through Invigorate

enemyKits.combo: Boreal Sweep slow → Hoarfrost root → Glacial Charge engage → Cryoseism stun ult
enemyKits.winCondition: chain frost CC (slow→root→stun) to lock the fight down
enemyKits.playWith: burst that converts her long CC chain into kills
enemyKits.playAgainst: she's a CC battery — cleanse/QSS the Cryoseism stun; spread to avoid the chain

enemyKits.combo: Riplash pull → Shock Therapy silence → Skewer suppress ult
enemyKits.winCondition: pull a target in and chain silence+suppress for a guaranteed kill
enemyKits.playWith: burst that deletes his pulled/suppressed target
enemyKits.playAgainst: respect Riplash range — stay spaced; cleanse/QSS the suppress

enemyKits.combo: Security Gate root → Sticky Mine → Tesla Dome zone ult
enemyKits.winCondition: root with the gate and trap a fight inside the Tesla Dome
enemyKits.passive: FDA stacks grant scaling magical power
enemyKits.playWith: frontline that holds enemies in her Dome
enemyKits.playAgainst: dodge the gate; spread vs the Dome — she's immobile, dive her

enemyKits.combo: Detection Protocol track → Chrono Swipe → Primeval Roar shield → Ursine Burst grab ult
enemyKits.winCondition: grab a key target out of position and burst them down
enemyKits.playWith: a team that collapses on his grabbed target
enemyKits.playAgainst: his grab ult is the whole threat — bait it / stay spaced; CC during Primeval Roar

enemyKits.combo: Scar mark → Obliterate homing missiles → Reckoning 1v1 realm ult
enemyKits.winCondition: Reckoning a target into the Nether Realm and crit them down 1v1
enemyKits.playWith: a team to clean up if his 1v1 stalls
enemyKits.playAgainst: the ult is a 1v1 — peel the realmed ally can't help, so don't be caught alone; he's crit-reliant early-weak
